title: A Mind Reader Is Headlining the White House Dinner — and Trump Might Be Part of the Act!
summary: Every year, there's a big fancy dinner in Washington, D.C., where journalists and politicians hang out. Usually, a comedian tells jokes and roasts (makes fun of) the president. But this year is different! The headliner is a mentalist — someone who seems to read minds. His name is Oz Pearlman, and he's famous for guessing people's PIN numbers and thoughts on TV. He even came in third on America's Got Talent in 2015! Pearlman says he wants to bring people together, not roast them. "My job is to make us laugh and have our jaws drop," he said. And here's the really cool part: President Donald Trump plans to attend — his first time at this dinner as president. Pearlman hinted that Trump might be part of the show! "Reading Donald Trump's mind is arguably the most impressive thing you could ever do," he said. Pearlman has been planning his big moment for 10 years. He hopes one jaw-dropping trick will make everyone talk about him for years. So, will he actually read Trump's mind? You'll have to wait and see!
why_it_matters: It shows that even serious events can be fun and surprising, and it reminds you that magic and mind-reading are all about practice and showmanship — not real superpowers.

background_read: Every year, journalists and politicians attend the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner in Washington, D.C.
Usually a comedian tells jokes and roasts the president, but this year it's different.
Mentalism is a type of magic where performers appear to read minds, but it's actually based on observation and suggestion.
Article_Structure: WHO: Oz Pearlman (mentalist) and President Donald Trump (attendee)
WHAT: The White House Correspondents' Association Dinner has a mentalist instead of a comedian as headliner
WHERE: Washington, D.C., at the White House dinner
WHY: To bring people together with amazing mind-reading tricks instead of roasting

perspectives.description: He wants to make the audience laugh and be amazed, and hopes to read Trump's mind.
He plans to attend and might take part in the act, which could be exciting or risky.
Some people might miss the usual comedian roasts, but others are curious about the new format.
